title: Monaco two-stop rule proxy
summary: Add a conservative special-case stop-debt proxy for Monaco races in years affected by mandatory multi-stop rules.
rationale: Several later run variants explored a Monaco rule-state override. External F1 reporting confirms Monaco received a mandatory strategy change requiring at least three tyre sets and at least two compounds in dry conditions, making race/year-specific stop debt potentially informative.
implementation_hint: When normalized `Race` indicates Monaco and `Year` matches the affected season, set a higher required observed-stop or tyre-set debt proxy. Combine it with current/prior observed `PitStop`, wet-weather exemption, race phase, and finish feasibility. Keep this as a small conditional block.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with low-medium confidence; useful only if matching rows exist in the dataset.
risk: Highly race/year-specific and potentially harmful if the synthetic task does not encode that rule; include a clear default fallback and avoid broad calendar assumptions.
